lts.lt_definition: I can identify and name body parts including private areas and explain which parts are private.
I can communicate my boundaries about physical contact and privacy needs.
I can apply foundational relationship skills and safety knowledge to build and maintain positive connections with others.
I can manage difficult feelings and reflect on kind behaviors when navigating challenging relationship situations.
The student enacts kindness, care, and respect for others as a sustained orientation across relationships.
I can identify and describe various family forms and their characteristics.
The student holds respectful attitudes toward different family forms and avoids stigmatising others based on family circumstances.
I can identify different forms of abuse and recognise when situations are unsafe or harmful.
I can explain personal rights, legal protections, and how to seek help when safety is compromised.
The student practises protective behaviours and actively seeks help to keep themselves and others safe.
I can identify and explain the key characteristics that make friendships positive, caring, and supportive.
I can apply strategies to build caring friendships and respond appropriately when friendships face challenges.
I can communicate assertively to express needs and set appropriate boundaries while managing my emotions effectively.
The student practises respectful communication and maintains healthy boundaries across diverse relationships and contexts.
I can identify different types of bullying and stereotypes and explain their harmful impacts.
I can challenge stereotypes and seek appropriate help when bullying occurs.
The student enacts responsibility to report bullying and support others when witnessing harmful behaviour.

lts.lt_name: Understanding Digital Risks and Safety Principles
lts.lt_definition: I can identify online risks and explain safety principles for protecting personal information and navigating digital relationships.

lts.lt_name: Evaluating and Responding to Online Threats
lts.lt_definition: I can critically evaluate online relationships and content, identify harmful situations, and apply appropriate response strategies.

lt_names: Understanding Digital Risks and Safety Principles
Evaluating and Responding to Online Threats
Understanding Digital Risks and Safety Principles
Evaluating and Responding to Online Threats
Understanding Digital Risks and Safety Principles

lts.lt_definition: The student practises cautious sharing of personal information and responsible handling of digital content across online contexts.

lt_names: Understanding Digital Risks and Safety Principles
Evaluating and Responding to Online Threats
Understanding Digital Risks and Safety Principles
Evaluating and Responding to Online Threats
Understanding Digital Risks and Safety Principles

lts.lt_definition: I can identify trustworthy versus untrustworthy people and recognise unsafe situations or inappropriate contact.
I can report safety concerns and seek appropriate help using proper vocabulary and channels.
The student practises trusting their instincts and maintains confidence when reporting safety concerns.
I can explain the key elements that create respectful, ethical romantic relationships including consent, boundaries, equality, and power dynamics.
I can evaluate how stereotypes, power imbalances, and cultural influences affect relationship dynamics and apply strategies for managing conflict and endings.
The student treats others with kindness and respect across all interactions while developing personal independence and interests.
I can recognise, respect and communicate consent and boundaries in relationships including early romantic and sexual relationships.
I can explain the principles of sexual consent including that it can be given, withheld or removed at any time.
I can explain contraceptive options, pregnancy choices, STI prevention, and reproductive health facts.
I can evaluate and signpost medically accurate sexual health information and counter misinformation effectively.
I can identify emotions, explain mental health concepts, and describe factors that support or harm wellbeing.

lt_names: Recognising and Responding to Mental Health Needs
Recognising and Responding to Mental Health Needs

lts.lt_name: Recognising and Responding to Mental Health Needs
lts.lt_definition: I can assess emotional responses, judge when support is needed, and communicate about mental health appropriately.

lt_names: Recognising and Responding to Mental Health Needs
Recognising and Responding to Mental Health Needs

lts.lt_definition: The student practises ongoing care for their own and others' mental health across different contexts.

lt_names: Recognising and Responding to Mental Health Needs
Recognising and Responding to Mental Health Needs

lts.lt_definition: I can identify the risks and benefits of online activities and their impact on wellbeing.
I can evaluate online content and relationships to make informed decisions about digital engagement.
I can identify key facts about nutrition, physical activity, substance use, hygiene, and health maintenance practices.
I can determine when and how to seek appropriate health support from trusted adults and healthcare professionals.
The student holds responsibility for maintaining their own health through regular self-examination and screening practices.
I can identify appropriate healthcare services for different needs and explain how to access them.
I can explain the legal framework for medical consent including age-related capacity and competency requirements.
I can identify potential hazards and assess safety risks across different environments and situations.

Applying Safety Knowledge and First Aid
Managing Personal Safety and Seeking Help
Managing Personal Safety and Risk Response

lts.lt_name: Managing Personal Safety and Seeking Help
lts.lt_definition: I can implement safety strategies, manage peer influence, and seek appropriate help when facing safety concerns.

lt_names: Managing Personal Safety and Peer Influence
Applying Safety Knowledge and First Aid
Managing Personal Safety and Seeking Help
Managing Personal Safety and Risk Response

lts.lt_definition: I can explain the physical changes that occur during puberty and adolescence.
I can identify when health issues require attention and apply strategies to protect wellbeing.
